title_original: Qrypt Brings Quantum-Secure Encryption to NVIDIA Jetson, Extending Protection From Edge AI to the Data Center
summary_original: Qrypt has launched its BLAST Protocol end-to-end quantum-secure encryption and quantum-entropy key generation on NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano and Jetson Thor, expanding quantum-secure protection from edge artificial intelligence endpoints to data centers (using NVIDIA BlueField devices). The BLAST Protocol, developed by Qrypt Chief Cryptographer Yevgeniy Dodis, enables identical encryption keys to be independently generated at each endpoint using quantum entropy sourced via exclusive agreements with Oak Ridge and Los Alamos National Laboratories, eliminating key transmission over networks. This approach addresses longstanding vulnerabilities in key-distribution architectures and mitigates “harvest now, decrypt later” threats. Qrypt integrates its quantum security stack across the NVIDIA platform, with custom kernel enhancements for security requirements ahead of official support. BLAST Protocol is now available through an early access integration program.
